title: Eulophia spectabilis
intro: Eulophia spectabilis, commonly known as the spectacular grass-pitcher orchid, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the orchid family (Orchidaceae). Traditionally, Eulophia spectabilis has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Eulophia spectabilis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Eulophia spectabilis is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, as well as being used in traditional remedies such as tonics and poultices.
taxonomy: Eulophia spectabilis is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This family is one of the largest and most diverse groups of flowering plants. Orchidaceae is further divided into 44 subfamilies, with Epidendroideae being the largest of these subfamilies. Eulophia spectabilis specifically falls under the subfamily Epidendroideae. The genus Eulophia is part of the subtribe Cribiinae within this subfamily.
